1	\chapter{Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf ($i=0$)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_FlatBaseChange}
3	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Section "Cohomology and
4	% base change, I", and Tag 02KH (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex and
5	% references/stacks-coherent.md).
6	
7	\section{Setup and motivation}
8	
9	This chapter establishes the $i=0$ (i.e.\ \(H^0\) / direct-image) case of flat
10	base change: the formation of the pushforward \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) of a
11	quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat base change on the target. It is the
12	foundational, fully self-contained slice of the cohomology-and-base-change
13	package that ultimately underlies relative representability statements; the
14	higher derived cases \(R^i f_*\) for \(i \geq 1\) are treated separately and are
15	out of scope here.
16	
17	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
18	quasi-separated, and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module.
19	A base change of \(f\) along an arbitrary morphism \(g : S' \to S\) is recorded
20	by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
21	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
22	\[
23	  \begin{array}{ccc}
24	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
25	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
26	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
27	  \end{array}
28	\]
29	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), and \(g' : X' \to X\) is the
30	first projection. We write \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) for the pullback
31	of \(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
32	
33	\section{The canonical base-change map}
34	
35	\begin{definition}\leanok
36	[Base-change map for the pushforward]
37	  \label{def:pushforward_base_change_map}
38	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap}
39	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Section "Cohomology and
40	  % base change, I", base-change diagram (\ref{equation-base-change-diagram})
41	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L877--904).
42	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
43	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent sheaf on $X$.
44	  % Suppose further that $g : S' \to S$ is any morphism of schemes. Denote
45	  % $X' = X_{S'} = S' \times_S X$ the base change of $X$ and denote
46	  % $f' : X' \to S'$ the base change of $f$.
47	  % Also write $g' : X' \to X$ the projection,
48	  % and set $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$."
49	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S\,Cohomology and base
50	  change, I.}
51	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
52	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(g : S' \to S\) an arbitrary morphism, giving the
53	  cartesian square above with projection \(g' : X' \to X\) and
54	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). The \emph{base-change map for the
55	  pushforward} is the canonical morphism of \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules
56	  \[
57	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \longrightarrow f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)
58	    = f'_*\mathcal{F}' .
59	  \]
60	  It is adjoint to the morphism
61	  \(f_*\mathcal{F} \to g_*\, f'_*\mathcal{F}' = f_*\,(g')_*\mathcal{F}'\) obtained
62	  by applying \(f_*\) to the unit
63	  \(\mathcal{F} \to (g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}\) of the
64	  \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction, using the commutativity
65	  \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\) of the square.
66	\end{definition}
67	
68	\section{The affine case}
69	
70	\subsection{Locality of isomorphisms for \texttt{Scheme.Modules} morphisms}
71	
72	Before treating the affine computation we record three project-local supplements
73	to Mathlib. Mathlib provides the per-open criterion
74	(\(\mathrm{IsIso}\,\varphi \iff \forall U,\ \mathrm{IsIso}(\varphi_U)\)) and a
75	stalkwise isomorphism criterion for \(\mathrm{TopCat.Sheaf}\)-valued morphisms, but
76	it does not package the stalk-local or basis-local criterion at the level of
77	morphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The following lemmas bridge that gap; they
78	are exactly the locality tools used in the first reduction of the affine
79	base-change lemma below, where one checks the base-change map after restricting to
80	the affine opens of the base. As bespoke infrastructure they carry no external
81	citation and stand on their proof sketches.
82	
83	\begin{lemma}\leanok
84	[Stalk-local criterion for isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
85	  \label{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk}
86	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_stalkFunctor_map}
87	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi : M \to N\) a morphism of sheaves of
88	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Then \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism if and only if the
89	  underlying morphism of abelian-group presheaves induces an isomorphism on the
90	  stalk at every point \(x \in X\).
91	\end{lemma}
92	
93	\begin{proof}
94	
95	  If \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism, then so is its image under the forgetful
96	  functor to abelian-group presheaves, and the stalk functor at each \(x\),
97	  being a functor, carries isomorphisms to isomorphisms. Conversely, assume the
98	  stalk map is an isomorphism at every \(x\). Package the underlying abelian-group
99	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\) as sheaves of abelian groups, and \(\varphi\) as a
100	  morphism of these sheaves; the stalkwise-isomorphism hypothesis lets us apply the
101	  \(\mathrm{TopCat.Sheaf}\)-level stalk-local isomorphism criterion to conclude that
102	  this morphism of sheaves of abelian groups is an isomorphism. Pushing it through
103	  the forgetful functor to presheaves yields an isomorphism of the underlying
104	  abelian-group presheaf morphisms, and since the forgetful functor from
105	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to abelian-group presheaves reflects isomorphisms,
106	  \(\varphi\) itself is an isomorphism.
107	\end{proof}
108	
109	\begin{lemma}\leanok
110	[Basis-local criterion for isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
111	  \label{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}
112	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis}
113	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk}
114	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(\{B_i\}_{i \in \iota}\) be a family of opens whose
115	  range is a basis of the topology of \(X\), and let \(\varphi : M \to N\) be a
116	  morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. If \(\varphi\) restricts to an
117	  isomorphism on the sections over every basic open \(B_i\), then \(\varphi\) is an
118	  isomorphism.
119	\end{lemma}
120	
121	\begin{proof}
122	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk}
123	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_stalk} it suffices to prove that the induced
124	  stalk map is bijective at each point \(x \in X\). For injectivity, a germ that
125	  vanishes at \(x\) already vanishes on some open neighbourhood, which may be
126	  shrunk to a basic open \(B_i\); on \(B_i\) the section map \(\varphi_{B_i}\) is
127	  injective, so the original section is zero, giving injectivity of the stalk map
128	  from injectivity over the basis. For surjectivity, an arbitrary germ at \(x\) is
129	  represented by a section of \(N\) over some basic open \(B_i\) containing \(x\);
130	  since \(\varphi_{B_i}\) is surjective, this section lifts to a section of \(M\)
131	  over \(B_i\), whose germ at \(x\) maps to the given germ. Thus the stalk map is
132	  bijective, and \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism.
133	\end{proof}
134	
135	\begin{lemma}\leanok
136	[Affine-open locality criterion for isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules]
137	  \label{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
138	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens}
139	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}
140	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi : M \to N\) a morphism of sheaves of
141	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Then \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism if and only if it
142	  restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of \(X\).
143	\end{lemma}
144	
145	\begin{proof}
146	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}
147	  The forward implication is immediate, since the section functor over a fixed open
148	  preserves isomorphisms. For the converse, the affine opens of a scheme form a
149	  basis of its topology, so the claim is the special case of
150	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis} in which the basis is the family of
151	  affine opens.
152	\end{proof}
153	
154	\subsection{The affine computation}
155	
156	The whole result rests on the following elementary computation in the affine
157	setting, where the base-change map is visibly an isomorphism because tensoring is
158	associative. Before stating it we isolate the one ingredient not yet available as
159	a ready-made result on which the affine reduction turns: the description of the
160	pushforward of a tilde-module along a morphism of affine schemes.
161	
162	We first record three bespoke \(\Gamma\)-fragment supports that compute the global
163	sections of an affine pushforward; they carry no external citation and stand on
164	their proof sketches.
165	
166	\begin{lemma}
167	
168	\leanok
169	[Naturality of the global-sections comparison square]
170	  \label{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
171	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
172	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings, and write
173	  \(\mathrm{gs}_R : \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \top) \cong R\) and
174	  \(\mathrm{gs}_{R'} : \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R', \top) \cong R'\) for the
175	  global-sections isomorphisms of the structure sheaves. Then the ring square
176	  \[
177	    \mathrm{gs}_R \;\circ\; (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{\sharp}_{\top}
178	    \;=\; \varphi \;\circ\; \mathrm{gs}_{R'}
179	  \]
180	  commutes, where \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{\sharp}_{\top}\) is the
181	  top-open component of the structure-sheaf comparison map underlying
182	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\).
183	\end{lemma}
184	
185	\begin{proof}
186	
187	  This is the naturality of the unit/counit of the
188	  \(\Gamma \dashv \operatorname{Spec}\) adjunction read off at the top open: the
189	  global-sections map of \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, under the
190	  global-sections isomorphisms on each side, conjugate to \(\varphi\) itself.
191	  Transposing the naturality square of the \(\operatorname{Spec}\) unit gives
192	  exactly the asserted ring equation.
193	\end{proof}
194	
195	\begin{lemma}
196	
197	\leanok
198	[Global sections of an affine pushforward]
199	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
200	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIso}
201	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
202	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let
203	  \(N\) be an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-module. Then there is a canonical
204	  isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
205	  \[
206	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\bigr)
207	    \;\cong\;
208	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma N\bigr),
209	  \]
210	  identifying the \(R\)-module of global sections of the pushforward with the
211	  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\) of the \(R'\)-module of global sections
212	  of \(N\).
213	\end{lemma}
214	
215	\begin{proof}
216	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
217	  Because \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}(\top) = \top\), the global sections of
218	  the pushforward \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\) are, on underlying abelian
219	  groups, the global sections of \(N\) with no transport along the open-set
220	  identification. The two sides therefore carry the same underlying abelian group;
221	  both unwind to nested restriction-of-scalars towers along the structure-sheaf
222	  global-sections maps. These towers are reconciled by applying the
223	  restriction-of-scalars composition identity twice and substituting the ring
224	  equation of
225	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}, which shows the
226	  resulting \(R\)-action is exactly restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\).
227	\end{proof}
228	
229	\begin{lemma}
230	
231	\leanok
232	[Global sections of an affine pushforward of a tilde-module]
233	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso}
234	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardTildeIso}
235	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
236	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
237	  be an \(R'\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
238	  \[
239	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \widetilde{M}\bigr)
240	    \;\cong\;
241	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi M .
242	  \]
243	\end{lemma}
244	
245	\begin{proof}
246	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
247	  Specialise Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso} to \(N = \widetilde{M}\) and
248	  compose with the tilde-unit isomorphism
249	  \(\Gamma(\widetilde{M}) \cong M\) of \(R'\)-modules (which restricts to an
250	  isomorphism of \(R\)-modules after applying \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi\)).
251	\end{proof}
252	
253	\begin{lemma}
254	
255	\leanok
256	[Sections of an affine pushforward over an arbitrary open]
257	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}
258	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIsoAt}
259	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
260	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings, let \(N\) be
261	  an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-module, and let \(U\) be an open of
262	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\), with preimage
263	  \(V = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}(U)\) in \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\). Then
264	  there is a canonical isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
265	  \[
266	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U\bigr)
267	    \;\cong\;
268	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, V)\bigr) .
269	  \]
270	  This is the open-indexed generalization of
271	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}, which is the case \(U = \top\) (so that
272	  \(V = \top\) as well). The family of these isomorphisms is natural in the open
273	  argument: the structure-sheaf restriction maps on the two sides commute with the
274	  isomorphisms; this naturality is recorded as a standalone statement in
275	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality}. For \(U = D(a)\) it is exactly
276	  the linear equivalence \(e_{D(a)}\) of movement~(1) in the proof of
277	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}.
278	\end{lemma}
279	
280	\begin{proof}
281	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
282	  The construction copies that of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso} verbatim,
283	  with the top open \(\top\) replaced throughout by the arbitrary open \(U\) (and
284	  its preimage \(V\)). The point that makes this replacement free is that the
285	  \(R\)-module structure on the sections is supplied \emph{uniformly} by
286	  restriction of scalars along the global-sections ring map at the top open,
287	  independently of the evaluation open; consequently both sides unwind to the same
288	  nested restriction-of-scalars towers, reconciled by applying the
289	  restriction-of-scalars composition identity twice and substituting the
290	  \(\top\)-level ring equation of
291	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}. Naturality in the open
292	  follows because every constituent of the construction is either a structure-sheaf
293	  restriction map or conjugation by a fixed ring map, and all of these commute with
294	  further restriction along an inclusion of opens.
295	\end{proof}
296	
297	\begin{lemma}
298	[Naturality in the open of the affine-pushforward section comparison]
299	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality}
300	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}
301	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(N\) be
302	  an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-module. Consider the two presheaves of
303	  \(R\)-modules on \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\), each contravariant in the open
304	  argument \(U \subseteq \operatorname{Spec}(R)\):
305	  \begin{itemize}
306	    \item the \emph{source presheaf}
307	      \(\mathcal{P} : U \mapsto \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U\bigr)\),
308	      whose restriction maps are the structure-sheaf restriction maps of the
309	      pushforward \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\);
310	    \item the \emph{target presheaf}
311	      \(\mathcal{Q} : U \mapsto
312	        \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U)\bigr)\),
313	      whose restriction maps are the structure-sheaf restriction maps of \(N\)
314	      transported through the preimage operation
315	      \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}\) and then read as \(R\)-linear maps by
316	      restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\).
317	  \end{itemize}
318	  Then the family \(\{e_U\}_U\) of \(R\)-linear isomorphisms of
319	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} (\(e_U\) being the isomorphism at the open
320	  \(U\)) is a \emph{natural} isomorphism \(\mathcal{P} \cong \mathcal{Q}\). Concretely,
321	  for any inclusion of opens \(U' \subseteq U\), with
322	  \(V = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U\) and
323	  \(V' = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U'\), the square
324	  \[
325	    \begin{array}{ccc}
326	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U\bigr)
327	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_U\;}
328	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, V)\bigr) \\[4pt]
329	      {\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}} \\[4pt]
330	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U'\bigr)
331	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_{U'}\;}
332	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, V')\bigr)
333	    \end{array}
334	  \]
335	  commutes, where the vertical maps are the respective structure-sheaf restriction
336	  maps. Equivalently, the isomorphisms \(e_U\) assemble into a single isomorphism of
337	  presheaves of \(R\)-modules, e.g.\ by exhibiting the components \(e_U\) as a
338	  natural transformation that is objectwise an isomorphism.
339	\end{lemma}
340	
341	\begin{proof}
342	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt, lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
343	  Each \(e_U\) is, by the construction of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}, a
344	  composite whose constituents are of exactly two kinds: structure-sheaf restriction
345	  maps (of \(N\), respectively of \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\)), and
346	  conjugation by a \emph{fixed} ring map — the one supplied by the
347	  restriction-of-scalars reconciliation and by the \(\top\)-level ring equation of
348	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}, neither of which depends
349	  on the open \(U\). Both kinds commute with further restriction along an inclusion
350	  \(U' \subseteq U\): restriction maps commute by the presheaf (functoriality) axiom
351	  of the two structure sheaves applied to the inclusions \(U' \subseteq U\) and
352	  \(V' \subseteq V\) (note \(V' = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U' \subseteq
353	  (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U = V\) since \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}\)
354	  is monotone), and conjugation by a fixed ring map commutes with restriction because
355	  the ring map is independent of the open. The naturality square is therefore the
356	  pasting of the three component-naturality squares of the constituents of \(e_U\):
357	  the (inverse-)naturality of the restriction-of-scalars composition reconciliation,
358	  the naturality of the canonical isomorphism induced by the top-open ring equation, and the
359	  naturality of the restriction-of-scalars composition for \(\varphi\). Each of these
360	  squares commutes for the structural reason just given, so their paste — the outer
361	  square — commutes. Since each \(e_U\) is moreover an isomorphism, the family is a
362	  natural isomorphism of the two presheaves.
363	\end{proof}
364	
365	\begin{lemma}
366	
367	\leanok
368	[The tilde restriction from the top open to a basic open is a localization]
369	  \label{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
370	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
371	  Let \(M\) be an \(R'\)-module and \(b \in R'\). Then the structure-sheaf
372	  restriction map
373	  \[
374	    \Gamma(\widetilde M,\, \top) \longrightarrow \Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(b)),
375	  \]
376	  read as a map of \(R'\)-modules, exhibits \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) as the
377	  localization of \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\). This is
378	  the \(R'\)-side localization fact transported in movement~(3) of the proof of
379	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}.
380	\end{lemma}
381	
382	\begin{proof}
383	  By the defining property of \(\widetilde{(-)}\), the tilde-localization map
384	  \(M \to \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) exhibits its target as the localization of
385	  \(M\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\). The global-sections map
386	  \(M \to \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) is the localization of \(M\) at the trivial
387	  submonoid \(\mathrm{powers}(1)\) — since \(D(1) = \top\) — and is therefore
388	  bijective. The triangle identity relating these two localization maps to the
389	  structure-sheaf restriction \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top) \to
390	  \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) then exhibits the restriction itself, post-composed
391	  with the inverse of the bijection \(M \cong \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\), as the
392	  localization of \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\).
393	\end{proof}

395	We record three further bespoke supports that drive the basic-open verification of
396	the affine-pushforward isomorphism below. They carry no external citation and stand
397	on their proof sketches.
398	
399	\begin{lemma}
400	
401	\leanok
402	[Restriction of scalars of a localized module]
403	  \label{lem:powers_restrictScalars}
404	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars}
405	  Let \(A\) be a commutative \(R\)-algebra, \(S \subseteq R\) a submonoid, and
406	  \(f : M \to N\) an \(A\)-linear map that exhibits \(N\) as the localization of
407	  \(M\) at the image submonoid
408	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S) \subseteq A\). Then the underlying \(R\)-linear map
409	  \(\operatorname{restr}_R f\) exhibits \(N\) as the localization of \(M\) at \(S\)
410	  itself.
411	\end{lemma}
412	
413	\begin{proof}
414	
415	  This is the exact converse of the standard fact that a localization of \(M\) at a
416	  submonoid \(S\) of \(R\) remains a localization after enlarging the base to an
417	  \(R\)-algebra \(A\) along the image submonoid \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S)\). One
418	  checks the three defining conditions of a localized module directly. Each
419	  \(s \in S\) acts invertibly on \(N\) because its image
420	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\) does and the two actions coincide by the scalar
421	  tower \(R \to A \to N\). Surjectivity of \(f\) up to denominators and the
422	  equality-witness condition transport along the map
423	  \(s \mapsto \operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\) from \(S\) onto
424	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S)\), again using
425	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\cdot{} = s\cdot{}\). In the application
426	  \(A = R'\), \(S = \mathrm{powers}(a)\), one has
427	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_{R'}(\mathrm{powers}(a)) = \mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\), so
428	  this identifies the localization of \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at \(a\)
429	  with the localization of \(M\) at \(\varphi a\); it is the ring-change core of the
430	  basic-open computation below.
431	\end{proof}
432	
433	\begin{lemma}
434	
435	\leanok
436	[Section-level counit isomorphism from a localization hypothesis]
437	  \label{lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized}
438	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule}
439	  Let \(N\) be an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-module and \(a \in R\). Suppose the
440	  structure-sheaf restriction map \(\Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\), read as
441	  an \(R\)-linear map of the global-section modules, exhibits \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) as
442	  the localization of \(\Gamma(N, \top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\). Then the
443	  tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit
444	  \(\mathrm{fromTilde}\Gamma\,N : \widetilde{\Gamma(N,\top)} \to N\) restricts to an
445	  isomorphism on the sections over the basic open \(D(a)\).
446	\end{lemma}
447	
448	\begin{proof}
449	
450	  Over \(D(a)\) the section map \(L\) of the counit sits in the triangle identity
451	  \(L \circ j = \rho\), where
452	  \(j : \Gamma(N,\top) \to \Gamma\bigl(\widetilde{\Gamma(N,\top)}, D(a)\bigr)\) is
453	  the tilde-localization map and \(\rho : \Gamma(N,\top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) is
454	  the structure-sheaf restriction. Both \(j\) and \(\rho\) exhibit their targets as
455	  the localization of \(\Gamma(N,\top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\) — \(j\) by the
456	  defining property of \(\widetilde{(-)}\), and \(\rho\) by hypothesis. By the
457	  uniqueness of localized modules there is a unique \(R\)-linear isomorphism \(e\)
458	  with \(e \circ j = \rho\); the triangle identity then forces \(L = e\), so \(L\) is
459	  an isomorphism. (This is the per-basic-open input feeding the basis-local
460	  criterion Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis}.)
461	\end{proof}
462	
463	\begin{lemma}
464	
465	\leanok
466	[Affine pushforward of a tilde-module, conditional form]
467	  \label{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional}
468	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule}
469	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso,
470	  lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized}
471	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and \(M\) an
472	  \(R'\)-module. Suppose that for every \(a \in R\) the structure-sheaf restriction
473	  of the pushforward \(N := (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M\) exhibits its
474	  sections over \(D(a)\) as the localization of its global sections at
475	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\). Then there is a canonical isomorphism of
476	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
477	  \[
478	    (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M \;\cong\;
479	    \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)} .
480	  \]
481	\end{lemma}
482	
483	\begin{proof}
484	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso,
485	  lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized}
486	  Write \(N := (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M\). Under the hypothesis,
487	  Lemma~\ref{lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized} applies for each \(a \in R\),

489	  sections over every basic open \(D(a)\). Since the basic opens form a basis of
490	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\), the basis-local criterion
491	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis} upgrades this to: the counit
492	  \(\mathrm{fromTilde}\Gamma\,N\) is an isomorphism, i.e.\ \(N\) lies in the
493	  essential image of \(\widetilde{(-)}\). Composing the inverse counit with the

497	
498	\begin{lemma}[Affine pushforward of a tilde-module]
499	  \label{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
500	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
501	% NOTE: the carrier-wall obstruction to `hloc` is resolved (restriction-of-scalars /
502	% scalar-tower instances feed lem:powers_restrictScalars; lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule
503	% supplies the R'-side localization). The single remaining obligation is the open-naturality of
504	% the section comparison e_{D(a)}, now stated as Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality};
505	% the proof sketch below derives each per-a hloc(a) from the top-level localization plus that
506	% naturality square for the inclusion D(a) ⊆ ⊤, so no section-level square is hand-proved per a.
507	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
508	  be an \(R'\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of
509	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
510	  \[
511	    \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\,\widetilde{M}
512	    \;\cong\;
513	    \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)},
514	  \]
515	  where \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi : \operatorname{Spec}(R') \to
516	  \operatorname{Spec}(R)\) is the induced morphism of affine schemes,
517	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\) denotes the quasi-coherent sheaf associated to a module, and
518	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) is the \(R\)-module obtained from \(M\) by
519	  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\). That is, pushing the quasi-coherent
520	  sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) forward along \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, up to
521	  this canonical identification, the tilde of the restriction of scalars of \(M\).
522	
523	  This is the classical fact that the direct image of a quasi-coherent sheaf along
524	  a morphism of affine schemes is computed on modules by restriction of scalars; it
525	  is the affine companion of the pullback formula (the affine case of the
526	  pushforward analogue of Stacks ``$\widetilde{M}$ and pullback''). It is recorded
527	  here as bespoke project infrastructure because the affine-pushforward
528	  identification does not appear as a ready-made result in the existing
529	  quasi-coherent sheaf theory.
530	\end{lemma}
531	
532	\begin{proof}
533	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso,
534	  lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional, lem:powers_restrictScalars,
535	  lem:gammaPushforwardIso, lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt,
536	  lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality,
537	  lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
538	  We build the isomorphism \emph{directly on a basis of basic opens}. This is the
539	  non-circular route: checking the comparison map on basic opens yields the object
540	  isomorphism, and quasi-coherence of the pushforward is then a corollary, not a
541	  prerequisite. (One must not reverse this order: the global-sections comparison
542	  \(\Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde{M}\bigr) \cong
543	  \operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) of
544	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso} alone would only upgrade to an object
545	  isomorphism once the pushforward is already known to be quasi-coherent, so
546	  quasi-coherence cannot be deduced from an object isomorphism built that way.)
547	
548	  \emph{The comparison morphism.} There is a canonical morphism of
549	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
550	  \[
551	    \alpha : \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)}
552	    \longrightarrow \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M},
553	  \]
554	  namely the tilde-adjoint of the global-sections identification of
555	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso}. We show \(\alpha\) is an isomorphism.
556	
557	  \medskip
558	  \emph{Reduction to basic opens.} The basic opens \(D(a)\), for \(a \in R\), form a
559	  basis of the topology of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\). By the basis-local criterion
560	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis} it suffices to check that \(\alpha\)
561	  restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over each basic open \(D(a)\).
562	
563	  \medskip
564	  \emph{The computation over \(D(a)\).} On the source, the sections of
565	  \(\widetilde{(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M)}\) over \(D(a)\) are the
566	  localization \(\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)[1/a]\) as an
567	  \(R[1/a]\)-module. On the target, since \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) =
568	  D(\varphi a)\), the sections of \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*
569	  \widetilde{M}\) over \(D(a)\) are the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over
570	  \(D(\varphi a)\), i.e.\ the localization \(M[1/\varphi a]\), regarded as an
571	  \(R[1/a]\)-module by restriction of scalars along the induced map
572	  \(R[1/a] \to R'[1/\varphi a]\). These two localizations agree: the element \(a\)
573	  acts on \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) through \(\varphi a\), so localizing
574	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at the multiplicative set generated by \(a\) is
575	  the same module as localizing \(M\) at the multiplicative set generated by
576	  \(\varphi a\). Both are the universal localization of
577	  \(M\) inverting \(\varphi a\), so the comparison map \(\alpha\) is an isomorphism
578	  on the sections over \(D(a)\). As this holds for every basic open, \(\alpha\) is
579	  an isomorphism.
580	
581	  \medskip
582	  \emph{The explicit formalization route over \(D(a)\).} The agreement of the two
583	  localizations just described is the entire content, but it must be realized
584	  through the global ring \(R\), because the \(R\)-action on the pushforward
585	  sections over \(D(a)\) is built from the global-sections module of
586	  \(N := (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M\) rather than directly from
587	  \(R[1/a]\). The reduction therefore proceeds exactly as the conditional
588	  assembly Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional} prescribes: it
589	  suffices to supply, for each \(a \in R\), the hypothesis \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) that
590	  the structure-sheaf restriction \(\Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) exhibits
591	  \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) as the localization of \(\Gamma(N, \top)\) at
592	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\). The \(R\)-action on \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) is the honest
593	  restriction-of-scalars action carried by the scalar tower \(R \to R' \to {-}\)
594	  determined by \(\varphi\); against that structure the ring-change converse
595	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is applicable, so the carrier of the
596	  \(R\)-module is exactly the one to which the localization claim refers, and no
597	  recursively-defined or ad-hoc section-level scalar action intervenes.
598	  We obtain \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) by transporting the already-known \(R'\)-side
599	  localization (Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}), in
600	  element-free fashion, across the open-indexed section comparison of
601	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} \emph{using its naturality in the open}
602	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality}). This proceeds in three
603	  movements.
604	
605	  \emph{(1) The \(D(a)\)-level linear equivalence.} This is the component at
606	  \(U = D(a)\) of the open-indexed family of
607	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}: the \(R\)-linear isomorphism
608	  \[
609	    e_{D(a)} : \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, D(a)\bigr)
610	      \;\cong\;
611	      \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(\varphi a))\bigr) ,
612	  \]
613	  using \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) = D(\varphi a)\), which is
614	  definitional. No fresh \(D(a)\)-level construction is needed: this is precisely the
615	  open-indexed isomorphism already supplied by
616	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} evaluated at the basic open \(D(a)\).
617	
618	  \emph{(2) The naturality square for \(D(a) \subseteq \top\).} The compatibility
619	  that relates \(e_{D(a)}\) to the \(\top\)-level isomorphism
620	  \(e_\top\) (the global-sections comparison of
621	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}) is precisely the open-naturality square of
622	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality}, instantiated at the inclusion
623	  \(D(a) \subseteq \top\): the square
624	  \[
625	    \begin{array}{ccc}
626	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, \top\bigr)
627	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_\top\;}
628	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, \top)\bigr) \\[4pt]
629	      {\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}}\big\downarrow & &
630	        \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)}} \\[4pt]
631	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, D(a)\bigr)
632	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_{D(a)}\;}
633	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(\varphi a))\bigr)
634	    \end{array}
635	  \]
636	  commutes, using \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) = D(\varphi a)\), which is
637	  definitional. Because the family \(\{e_U\}_U\) is a natural isomorphism, this single
638	  square is available uniformly in \(a\); there is no per-\(a\) section-level identity
639	  to re-prove by hand. It intertwines the source restriction
640	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}\) on the pushforward with the (restriction-of-scalars
641	  reading of the) target restriction \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)}\) on
642	  \(\widetilde M\).
643	
644	  \emph{(3) Discharging \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) by transport.} The target restriction
645	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)} : \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top) \to
646	  \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(\varphi a))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, D(\varphi a)) =
647	  M[1/\varphi a]\) as the \(\mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\)-localization of
648	  \(M = \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) over \(R'\); this is exactly
649	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}. Apply the ring-change converse
650	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} (with \(R\)-algebra \(A = R'\) and submonoid
651	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\), so that
652	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_{R'}(\mathrm{powers}(a)) = \mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\)) —
653	  its \(\operatorname{Algebra} R\, R'\) and scalar-tower hypotheses being supplied by
654	  the restriction-of-scalars structure along \(\varphi\) — to read this same map as
655	  the \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\)-localization over \(R\). The commuting naturality square
656	  of movement (2) intertwines this target restriction with the source restriction
657	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)} : \Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) by way of the
658	  isomorphisms \(e_\top\) and \(e_{D(a)}\); transporting the localization property
659	  across that square — via the transport-along-an-equivalence principle for localized
660	  modules — yields that \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}\) is the
661	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\)-localization on \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) over \(R\), i.e.\
662	  \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\). No section-level computation specific to the individual \(a\)
663	  is performed: the only \(a\)-dependent input is the instantiation of the single
664	  natural-isomorphism square at \(D(a)\). Feeding the family
665	  \(\{\mathrm{hloc}(a)\}_{a \in R}\) to the conditional assembly
666	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional} produces the unconditional
667	  object isomorphism \(\alpha\).
668	
669	  \medskip
670	  \emph{Naturality in the open drives the transport.} The comparison assembled
671	  here, \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \circ \widetilde{(-)} \cong
672	  \widetilde{(-)} \circ \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\), is natural in \emph{both}
673	  arguments — in the module \(M\) and in the open \(U\). The naturality in the
674	  module is the usual functoriality; the naturality in the open is the content of
675	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality}: the family \(\{e_U\}_U\) of
676	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} commutes with the structure-sheaf
677	  restriction maps on both sides. It is precisely this open-naturality that drives
678	  the localization transport \emph{uniformly}: the per-\(a\) localization fact
679	  \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) follows from the \(\top\)-level localization
680	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule} together with the naturality
681	  square of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt_naturality} for the inclusion
682	  \(D(a) \hookrightarrow \top\), rather than from a section-level naturality square
683	  re-proved by hand for each individual \(a\). This is the single remaining
684	  obligation of the construction; the carrier of the \(R\)-action on the pushforward
685	  sections is the restriction-of-scalars structure along \(\varphi\), against which
686	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} applies directly.
687	
688	  The \(R\)-module structure on the \(R'\)-side sections to which the ring-change
689	  converse Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is applied is the honest
690	  restriction-of-scalars structure: \(R\) acts through \(\varphi\), i.e.\ via the
691	  \(R\)-algebra structure on \(R'\) determined by \(\varphi\) and the associated
692	  scalar tower \(R \to R' \to M[1/\varphi a]\). It is \emph{not} an ad-hoc,
693	  recursively-defined scalar action. Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is
694	  stated against exactly this restriction-of-scalars / scalar-tower structure, and
695	  it is under that structure that localizing \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at
696	  \(a\) coincides with localizing \(M\) at \(\varphi a\).
697	
698	  % NOTE: This lemma is the affine case of the general fact that the pushforward of
699	  % a quasi-coherent sheaf along an affine morphism is quasi-coherent. In current
700	  % Mathlib master that fact is the IsLocalizing / fromTildeGamma characterization
701	  % (AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_pushforward); it post-dates the project's
702	  % pinned Mathlib, so the project proves it in-tree by the basic-open route above.
703	  % The in-tree proof remains valid and is not made redundant by a future Mathlib
704	  % bump.
705	  \medskip
706	  \emph{Quasi-coherence as a corollary.} The object isomorphism just constructed
707	  exhibits \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M} \cong
708	  \widetilde{(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M)}\) as the tilde of a module. Tilde
709	  modules are quasi-coherent and quasi-coherence of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
710	  is closed under isomorphism, so the pushforward
711	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent. This
712	  conclusion is stated \emph{after} the object isomorphism, of which it is a
713	  consequence — no separate ``pushforward preserves quasi-coherent'' input is
714	  needed for the affine lane.
715	\end{proof}
716	
717	\begin{remark}
718	  This single isomorphism discharges both inputs that the affine reduction below
719	  requires of the pushforward of a tilde-module.
720	  \begin{enumerate}
721	    \item \emph{The global-sections comparison.} Applying the global-sections
722	      (module-of-sections) functor to the isomorphism identifies the
723	      \(R\)-module of sections of
724	      \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) with
725	      \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\); this is exactly the \(\Gamma\)-fragment
726	      comparison used to recognise the section-level base-change map.
727	    \item \emph{Quasi-coherence of the pushforward.} The tilde of any module is
728	      quasi-coherent, and quasi-coherence of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules is
729	      closed under isomorphism; hence
730	      \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent.
731	      In particular no separate ``pushforward preserves quasi-coherent'' theorem is
732	      needed for the affine lane.
733	  \end{enumerate}
734	  Because both inputs the affine reduction asks of the pushforward are corollaries
735	  of this single object isomorphism — the section-level module identification and
736	  the quasi-coherence of the pushforward — this lemma is the sole bespoke
737	  ingredient the affine lane requires.
738	\end{remark}
739	
740	\begin{lemma}\leanok
741	[Affine base change]
742	  \label{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}
743	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso}
744	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map}

753	  % (\ref{equation-base-change-diagram})
754	  % we have
755	  % $$
756	  % g^*f_*\mathcal{F} = f'_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}.
757	  % $$"
758	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
759	  change''.}
760	  Assume \(f : X \to S\) is an affine morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a

762	  above the canonical map of
763	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism
764	  \[
765	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\cong\; f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr).
766	  \]
767	  The quasi-coherence hypothesis on \(\mathcal{F}\) is essential and is not a mere

771	  \(\widetilde{M}\); without it neither the affine description of the pushforward
