1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.

83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.

32

34	  `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackFree` (`.lake/.../PullbackFree.lean`):
35	  - `pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ := ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ).homEquiv _ _).symm
36	    (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ)` — i.e. it IS the adjunction-mate (conjugate) of the
37	    concrete pushforward-side map `unitToPushforwardObjUnit`. (PullbackFree.lean, the
38	    `noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnit` block.)
39	  - `instance [F.Final] : IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ)` — proved via
40	    `isIso_iff_coyoneda_map_bijective` + `bijective_pushforwardSections` (finality ⇒
41	    sections bijective). Its full instance-arg list, from the hover, is
42	    `[Functor.IsContinuous F J K] [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
43	    [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)] [(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]
44	    [F.Final]`.
45	  These are NOT design noise: `IsContinuous`/`HasSheafCompose`/`IsRightAdjoint` are exactly
46	  the data needed to *define* `pushforward`/`pullback`/the adjunction in the first place,
47	  and `F.Final` is the genuine mathematical hypothesis. This is the standard Mathlib shape
48	  for an adjunction-mate comparison that becomes iso under a hypothesis. The closest direct
49	  precedents:
50	  - `CategoryTheory.Functor.Monoidal.μIso` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Functor`):
51	    the monoidal-functor lax-comparison `μ : F X ⊗ F Y ⟶ F (X ⊗ Y)` (itself an

53	    hypothesis `[F.Monoidal]`, with `μIso_hom : (μIso F X Y).hom = LaxMonoidal.μ F X Y`.
54	    Mathlib does NOT publish a bare `instance : IsIso (LaxMonoidal.μ …)` and reason about it
55	    on composites — it hands out the `Iso`.

57	    `(asIso (sigmaComparison _ _)).symm ≪≫ Sigma.mapIso (fun _ ↦ asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ))`

72	### Decision: canonical idiom for transporting `IsIso` across the coherence equation
73	
74	- **Mathlib idiom**: *Bundle the comparison as an `Iso` (`asIso`) at a clean construction
75	  site, then do ALL downstream reasoning at the `Iso` level* — never re-synthesize the
76	  `F.Final`-keyed `IsIso` instance on a `rw`-produced composite. This is precisely what
77	  `μIso` (hand out the iso, expose `μIso_hom`) and `pullbackObjFreeIso`
78	  (`asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ)` + `Sigma.mapIso`/`≪≫`) do. Concretely, for the project's
79	  cancellation goal "given `IsIso (A ≫ B ≫ C)` with `A`, `C` iso, deduce `IsIso B`":
80	  1. Build the two outer legs as named `Iso`s where their instances are clean:
81	     `iA := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).symm.app _` (a `NatIso` component — its
82	     `.hom`/`.inv` are iso UNCONDITIONALLY via `Iso.isIso_hom`/`Iso.isIso_inv`, no `Final`),
83	     and `iC := asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit V.ι.toRingCatSheafHom)` (built ONCE in a local
84	     `have`, freezing the `IsRightAdjoint`/`Final` implicits into the term).
85	  2. Reason with `iA.hom`, `iC.hom`, and `(Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).mapIso _` whose
86	     `IsIso` instances are the global, unconditional `CategoryTheory.Iso.isIso_hom` /
87	     `Functor.mapIso`-based ones — TC never re-enters `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`'s implicit
88	     args.
89	  3. Cancel with `CategoryTheory.IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left`
90	     (`[IsIso f] [IsIso (f ≫ g)] ⊢ IsIso g`, `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Iso`) for the left leg
91	     then `IsIso.of_isIso_comp_right` for the right leg.
92	  The point: `asIso e` requires `[IsIso e]` only at the moment of construction (a single,
93	  clean local goal whose only relevant in-scope instance is the matching `(Opens.map _).Final`),
94	  and thereafter `(asIso e).hom`'s iso-ness comes from `Iso.isIso_hom`, which is a global
95	  instance with no `Final`/`IsRightAdjoint` implicit to clash with a stale local `haveI`.
96	- **Why the project's current `rw + infer_instance` fails**: `pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ`'s
97	  instance args include the `Prop`-valued `[(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]` and the
98	  `[F.Final]` proof. A pre-established `haveI : IsIso (pbu g)` head-matches the goal's
99	  `IsIso (pbu ?)`, so Lean's resolution *picks the local hypothesis first*, then fails to
100	  unify the buried implicit instance terms (defeq but not syntactically equal at reducible
101	  transparency), and — because local instances are tried with priority and limited
102	  backtracking — resolution aborts rather than falling through to the global `OfFinal`
103	  instance. Bundling into `asIso`/`Iso` removes every such `IsIso (pbu ?)` synthesis goal
104	  downstream, so the clash can never arise.
105	- **Gap**: divergent-with-(small)-cost. The project should adopt the bundled-iso idiom; the
106	  cost of NOT doing so is the current hard block (the math is complete, the proof can't close).
107	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB (proof-tactic alignment, not a signature refactor).
108	
109	### Decision: recurrence in Phase 2/3
110	
111	- **Mathlib idiom**: same `asIso`/bundled-`Iso` discipline applies to every adjunction-mate
112	  comparison. `μIso` is the template for *all* of monoidal-functor comparison reasoning in
113	  Mathlib; the project's Phase-2 `pullbackObjTensorToTensor` is the direct analogue of
114	  `μ`/`μIso` for the `f^*` pseudofunctor.
115	- **Project's current path**: Phase 2 plans to prove `pullbackObjTensorToTensor` iso by the
116	  same finality chart-chase; Phase 3 composes `pullbackTensorIso⁻¹ ≫ f^*e ≫ pullbackUnitIso`.
117	- **Gap**: Phase 2 will hit the SAME wall *iff* it again routes through
118	  `rw [coherence]; infer_instance`. Phase 3 will NOT — it composes already-bundled `Iso`s
119	  (`pullbackTensorIso`, `pullbackUnitIso`), which is iso-level reasoning by construction.
120	- **Cost of divergence (if any)**: if the project treats Decision 2 as a one-off
121	  type-ascription hack rather than adopting the bundled-iso idiom as the *standard pattern*,
122	  Phase 2 re-pays the same debugging cost. Adopting it once (a `pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso`
123	  thin wrapper mirroring `μIso`, plus an `Iso`-level coherence mirroring `μIso_hom`) makes
124	  Phase 2 mechanical.
125	- **Verdict**: PROCEED — the *shape* (signature) is NOT the bottleneck, so there is nothing
126	  to refactor upstream; the recurrence is real but is fully absorbed by the Decision-2 idiom
127	  applied uniformly.

129	## Recommendation
130	The signature is Mathlib's and is canonical (it mirrors `Functor.Monoidal.μIso`); do not
131	attempt to refactor the comparison-map API. The block is a Lean typeclass-resolution accident
132	on `rw`-produced composites, triggered by stale local `haveI : IsIso (pbu …)` hypotheses whose
133	buried `IsRightAdjoint`/`Final` implicits fail to unify. Fix it the way Mathlib does in this
134	very file: introduce a thin bundled wrapper `pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso φ [F.Final] := asIso
135	(pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ)` (the analogue of `μIso`), build each leg of the coherence as a named
136	`Iso` at a clean site, and do all `IsIso` cancellation through `Iso.isIso_hom`/`Functor.mapIso`/
137	`IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left`/`of_isIso_comp_right` — never via `infer_instance` on a `pbu`
138	composite. Apply the same discipline to Phase 2's `pullbackObjTensorToTensor`; Phase 3 is
139	already iso-level and unaffected. This is a uniform proof idiom, not a one-time workaround and
140	not a signature change.

2798	  \emph{mate} of the pushforward comparison \(\mathrm{upu}\,f\)

2800	  and similarly for \(h\) and \(h \mathbin{;} f\). Since the mate correspondence is a
2801	  bijection, the asserted identity follows once both sides are transposed under the
2802	  adjunction \((h \mathbin{;} f)^* \dashv (h \mathbin{;} f)_*\).

2804	  Under this transpose the left-hand side \(\mathrm{pbu}(h \mathbin{;} f)\) becomes the
2805	  pushforward comparison \(\mathrm{upu}(h \mathbin{;} f)\). The right-hand side, a
2806	  composite involving \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{inv}\) and the mates of
2807	  \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\) and \(\mathrm{pbu}\,h\), transposes --- using the conjugation of the
2808	  pseudofunctoriality isomorphisms (\(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f\) on the left adjoints
2809	  corresponds to \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,h\,f\) on the right adjoints) and the unit of
2810	  the composite adjunction \((f^* \dashv f_*) \circ (h^* \dashv h_*)\) --- exactly into the
2811	  right-hand side of the pushforward coherence
2812	  \cref{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}, namely
2813	  \(\mathrm{upu}\,f \mathbin{;} f_*(\mathrm{upu}\,h) \mathbin{;}
2814	  (\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{hom}\). Thus the two transposed sides coincide
2815	  by \cref{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}, and the original identity follows by the

2819	\begin{lemma}
2820	[Pullback preserves the structure sheaf]
2821	  \label{lem:pullback_unit_iso}
2822	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso}
2823	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2824	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes. The pullback of the structure sheaf
2825	  is the structure sheaf: there is a canonical isomorphism
2826	  \[
2827	    f^*\bigl(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\bigr)
2828	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2829	    \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,Y.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf},
2830	    \qquad\text{i.e.}\qquad
2831	    f^* \mathcal{O}_X \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_Y,
2832	  \]
2833	  where \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf} = \mathcal{O}_X\) is
2834	  the monoidal unit of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\).
2835	\end{lemma}

2837	\begin{proof}
2838	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp,
2839	        lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}
2840	  This is the cheap half, reachable directly. The canonical comparison map is the
2841	  genuine Mathlib morphism
2842	  \[
2843	    \mathtt{SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,f :
2844	      f^*\mathcal{O}_X \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_Y,
2845	  \]
2846	  the unit comparison \(\eta\) of \(f^*\); abbreviate it \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\). Mathlib
2847	  already proves this map is an isomorphism whenever the comparison functor is
2848	  \(\mathtt{Final}\): the instance
2849	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal}\). This instance is
2850	  used in the proof of the open-immersion case
2851	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial.pullback}\): for an open immersion the comparison functor
2852	  is \(\mathtt{Final}\) outright, so \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\) is an
2853	  isomorphism on the nose.
2854	
2855	  For a \emph{general} morphism \(f\) the comparison functor need not be
2856	  \(\mathtt{Final}\) globally, so the isomorphism is established by reduction to
2857	  local charts. Cover \(Y\) by affine charts; on such a chart \(V \subseteq Y\)
2858	  factor the inclusion \(V.\iota \mathbin{;} f = g \mathbin{;} U.\iota\) through
2859	  \(g = f.\mathtt{resLE}\,U\,V\), whose comparison functor
2860	  \(\mathtt{Opens.map}\,g.\mathtt{base}\) \emph{is} \(\mathtt{Final}\) (via
2861	  \(\mathtt{final\_of\_representablyFlat}\)).

2863	  The composition coherence that ties the comparison of a composite to the comparisons
2864	  of its factors is now established: it is the pullback-side identity
2865	  \[
2866	    \mathrm{pbu}(V.\iota \mathbin{;} f)
2867	      \;=\;
2868	    (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,V.\iota\,f).\mathrm{inv} \;\mathbin{;}\;
2869	      (\mathtt{pullback}\,V.\iota).\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\bigr) \;\mathbin{;}\;
2870	      \mathrm{pbu}\,(V.\iota)
2871	  \]
2872	  of \cref{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}, obtained by adjunction-mate transport from
2873	  the pushforward-side coherence \cref{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}. What is
2874	  reused from the open-immersion case is therefore only: the per-chart factorization
2875	  \(V.\iota \mathbin{;} f = g \mathbin{;} U.\iota\) with \(g = f.\mathtt{resLE}\,U\,V\),
2876	  the \(\mathtt{Final}\)-chart instance
2877	  \(\mathtt{instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal}\) (which makes
2878	  \(\mathrm{pbu}\) for an open immersion or a \(\mathtt{Final}\)-chart morphism an
2879	  isomorphism), and the project globalizer \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_isIso\_restrict}\)
2880	  (the same one used in \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}).
2881	
2882	  The sole remaining step is to assemble these into the global isomorphism. On each
2883	  chart the coherence equation exhibits \(\mathrm{pbu}(V.\iota \mathbin{;} f)\) as a
2884	  composite of three isomorphisms --- the pseudofunctoriality iso
2885	  \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,V.\iota\,f).\mathrm{inv}\), the image under
2886	  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,V.\iota\) of the \(\mathtt{Final}\)-chart isomorphism
2887	  \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\) (here \(f\) is the \(\mathtt{Final}\)-chart morphism \(g\) after
2888	  the factorization), and the open-immersion isomorphism \(\mathrm{pbu}\,(V.\iota)\) ---
2889	  hence \(\mathrm{pbu}(V.\iota \mathbin{;} f)\) is itself an isomorphism on every chart.
2890	  Transporting this per-chart isomorphism across
2891	  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\) identifies it with the restriction of the
2892	  global comparison, and the globalizer \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_isIso\_restrict}\) upgrades
2893	  ``iso on every chart'' to the global statement. The result is the asserted
2894	  isomorphism \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\).
2895	\end{proof}

130	def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) : Type (u+1) :=

139	lemma OnProduct.isLocallyTrivial {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
140	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : IsLocallyTrivial L.carrier := L.2

142	/-- **Stacks 01HH (pullback of an invertible sheaf is invertible).** For any
143	morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and any locally-trivial sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules
144	`M`, the pulled-back module `f^* M : Y.Modules` is again locally trivial.
145	
146	The proof unfolds an affine open cover trivialising `M`, pulls back along `f`,
147	and observes that `f^*` commutes with restriction along open immersions (the
148	`pullback`/`restrictFunctor` natural isomorphism in
149	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) so that the trivialising chart for
150	`M|_U` becomes a trivialising chart for `(f^* M)|_(f⁻¹ U)`. The full chart-chase
151	involves the natural-isomorphism `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` plus the
152	preservation of `SheafOfModules.unit` under pullback (Mathlib
153	`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackFree`'s
154	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit`). Iter-187 wraps this chase as a named typed sorry
155	(helper-budget = 1); iter-188+ instantiates the chart-chase. -/
156	lemma IsLocallyTrivial.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) {M : X.Modules}
157	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
158	    IsLocallyTrivial ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) := by
159	  intro y
160	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, eM⟩ := hM (f.base y)
161	  have hyU' : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := hxU
162	  obtain ⟨V, hV_aff, hyV, hVU⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyU'
163	  refine ⟨V, hyV, hV_aff, ?_⟩
164	  -- Stacks 01HH chart-chase: factor `V.ι ≫ f` through `U` using
165	  -- `Scheme.Hom.resLE`, then transport through `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
166	  -- and `pullbackComp` to identify the pullback at `V` with the pullback of
167	  -- the unit on `U` to `V`. Close via `eM` and `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
168	  -- (which is an iso since `Opens.map g.base` is final via the
169	  -- `RepresentablyFlat` instance).
170	  obtain ⟨eM⟩ := eM
171	  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
172	  have hg_comp : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
173	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final :=
174	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
175	  refine ⟨?_⟩
176	  -- 1: ((pullback f).obj M).restrict V.ι ≅ (pullback V.ι).obj ((pullback f).obj M)
177	  let i1 :=
178	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M)
179	  -- 2: ≅ (pullback (V.ι ≫ f)).obj M
180	  let i2 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).app M
181	  -- 3: ≅ (pullback (g ≫ U.ι)).obj M  (using V.ι ≫ f = g ≫ U.ι)
182	  let i3 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hg_comp.symm).app M
183	  -- 4: ≅ (pullback g).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
184	  let i4 := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).symm).app M
185	  -- 5: ≅ (pullback g).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
186	  let i5 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso
187	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M)
188	  -- 6: ≅ (pullback g).obj (unit_U)
189	  let i6 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso eM
190	  -- 7: ≅ unit_V  (pullback of unit is unit via the Mathlib morphism, which is
191	  -- iso since the underlying `Opens.map g.base` is Final)
192	  let i7 := asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
193	  exact i1 ≪≫ i2 ≪≫ i3 ≪≫ i4 ≪≫ i5 ≪≫ i6 ≪≫ i7
194	
195	/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection
196	
197	For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
198	pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
199	open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
200	module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
201	projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
202	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
203	functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
204	the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
205	invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
208	01HH). -/
209	
210	/-- **Pullback along the projection** `π_T : C ×_S T ⟶ T` of a relative
211	curve. Sends a *line bundle on `T`* — i.e. an `O_T`-module `N` equipped with a
212	proof `hN : IsLocallyTrivial N` — to the line bundle `π_T^* N` on `C ×_S T`,
213	induced by the `Scheme.Modules.pullback` functor on `Scheme.Modules` together
214	with the preservation step `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH).
215	
216	iter-187: refined to consume the `OnProduct`-style subtype on the source: the
217	underlying carrier is the direct application of Mathlib's
218	`Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)` functor at `N`, and the
219	locally-trivial witness comes from the project-side helper
220	`IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH, named typed sorry pending the affine
221	chart chase). -/
222	noncomputable def pullbackAlongProjection {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
223	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (N : T.Modules) (hN : IsLocallyTrivial N) :
224	    OnProduct πC πT :=
225	  ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N,
226	    hN.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)⟩

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate

open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance

private lemma isIso_restrict_pbu {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    (U : X.Opens) (V : Y.Opens) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor V.ι).map
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)) := by
  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
  have hg_comp : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
  haveI hgF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI hUF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map U.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI hVF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map V.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  -- Step 1: IsIso (pbu (g ≫ U.ι))
  have hgU_iso : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (g ≫ U.ι).toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    rw [pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp g U.ι]
    have e2 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).map
        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit U.ι.toRingCatSheafHom)) := by
      letI := isIso_pbu_of_final U.ι
      exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
    have e3 : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit U.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
      isIso_pbu_of_final U.ι
    have e1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).inv.app
        (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := inferInstance
    have e4 : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
      isIso_pbu_of_final g
    haveI := e1; haveI := e2; haveI := e4
    infer_instance
  -- transport to pbu (V.ι ≫ f)
  rw [hg_comp] at hgU_iso
  -- Step 2: cancel outer legs in pbu (V.ι ≫ f) = pullbackComp.inv ≫ (pullback V.ι).map (pbu f) ≫ pbu V.ι
  rw [pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp V.ι f] at hgU_iso
  haveI hVfcomp := hgU_iso
  haveI ha : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).inv.app
      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := inferInstance
  haveI hc : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit V.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    isIso_pbu_of_final V.ι
  have hbc : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit V.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left _ _
  haveI := hbc
  have hb : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)) :=
    IsIso.of_isIso_comp_right _ _
  -- transport back across restrictFunctorIsoPullback
  exact (NatIso.isIso_map_iff (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι)
    (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)).mpr hb

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance

private lemma isIso_restrict_pbu {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    (U : X.Opens) (V : Y.Opens) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor V.ι).map
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)) := by
  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
  have hg_comp : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
  haveI hgF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI hUF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map U.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI hVF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map V.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  have hgU_iso : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (g ≫ U.ι).toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    rw [pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp g U.ι]
    have iA : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).inv.app
        (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := inferInstance
    have iC : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
      isIso_pbu_of_final g
    have iB : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).map
        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit U.ι.toRingCatSheafHom)) := by
      have := isIso_pbu_of_final U.ι
      exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
    exact @IsIso.comp_isIso _ _ _ _ _ _ _ iA (@IsIso.comp_isIso _ _ _ _ _ _ _ iB iC)
  rw [hg_comp] at hgU_iso
  rw [pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp V.ι f] at hgU_iso
  haveI ha : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).inv.app
      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := inferInstance
  haveI hc : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit V.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    isIso_pbu_of_final V.ι
  haveI hVfcomp := hgU_iso
  have hbc : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit V.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left _ _
  haveI := hbc
  have hb : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)) :=
    IsIso.of_isIso_comp_right _ _
  exact (NatIso.isIso_map_iff (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι)
    (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)).mpr hb

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

import Mathlib

open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance

example {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    (U : X.Opens) (V : Y.Opens) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) : True := by
  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
  haveI hgF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI hUF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map U.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  -- build the step-1 iso
  let iA := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).symm.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
  let iB := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso
    (asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit U.ι.toRingCatSheafHom))
  let iC := asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
  let iso_gU := iA ≪≫ iB ≪≫ iC
  have hgU_iso : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (g ≫ U.ι).toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    rw [pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp g U.ι]
    exact iso_gU.isIso_hom
  trivial

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate

open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

/-- Bundled `Iso` form of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` for a `Final` comparison functor. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)

@[simp] lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso_hom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    (pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g).hom = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom :=
  rfl

example {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    (U : X.Opens) (V : Y.Opens) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) : True := by
  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
  haveI hgF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI hUF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map U.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  let iA := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).symm
  let iB := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso U.ι)
  let iC := pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g
  have hgU_iso : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (g ≫ U.ι).toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    rw [pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp g U.ι]
    exact (iA ≪≫ iB ≪≫ iC).isIso_hom
  trivial

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

    (isIso_pbu_of_final g)

    (U : X.Opens) (V : Y.Opens) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor V.ι).map
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)) := by
  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
  have hg_comp : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
  haveI hgF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI hUF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map U.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI hVF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map V.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  -- Step 1: pbu (g ≫ U.ι) is iso (Iso-level composite via the coherence)
  have hgU_iso : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (g ≫ U.ι).toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    rw [pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp g U.ι]
    let iA := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).symm
    let iB := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso U.ι)
    let iC := pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g
    exact (iA ≪≫ iB ≪≫ iC).isIso_hom
  -- transport to pbu (V.ι ≫ f), then cancel the outer iso legs
  rw [hg_comp, pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp V.ι f] at hgU_iso
  haveI hVfcomp := hgU_iso
  haveI ha : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).inv.app
      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := inferInstance
  haveI hc : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit V.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    isIso_pbu_of_final V.ι
  haveI hbc : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map

      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit V.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left _ _

      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)) :=
    IsIso.of_isIso_comp_right _ _
  exact (NatIso.isIso_map_iff (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι)
    (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)).mpr hb

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

  haveI hUF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map U.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI hVF : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map V.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  have hgU_iso : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (g ≫ U.ι).toRingCatSheafHom) := by

    let iB := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso U.ι)
    let iC := pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g
    exact (iA ≪≫ iB ≪≫ iC).isIso_hom

741	(they match the blueprint statement). -/
742	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules}
743	    (_hM : IsInvertible M) (_hN : IsInvertible N) (_hP : IsInvertible P) :
744	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) :=
745	  tensorObj_assoc_iso
746	
747	/-- **Middle-four interchange for `⊗_X`** (helper). For arbitrary `𝒪_X`-modules
748	`A, B, C, D`, there is an isomorphism `(A ⊗ B) ⊗ (C ⊗ D) ≅ (A ⊗ C) ⊗ (B ⊗ D)`,
749	assembled from the unconditional associator and the braiding (no coherence consumed).
750	Used to reassociate the four factors in `IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
751	private noncomputable def tensorObj_middleFour {X : Scheme.{u}} (A B C D : X.Modules) :
752	    tensorObj (tensorObj A B) (tensorObj C D)
753	      ≅ tensorObj (tensorObj A C) (tensorObj B D) :=
754	  tensorObj_assoc_iso ≪≫
755	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl A)
756	      (tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
757	        tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding B C) (Iso.refl D) ≪≫
758	        tensorObj_assoc_iso) ≪≫
759	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm
760	
761	/-- **Step 3 — `⊗`-invertibility is closed under tensor product** (blueprint
762	`lem:isinvertible_tensor`). If `M, M'` are `⊗`-invertible with inverses `N, N'`,
763	then `N ⊗ N'` is a tensor inverse of `M ⊗ M'`. -/
764	theorem IsInvertible.tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules}
765	    (hM : IsInvertible M) (hM' : IsInvertible M') :
766	    IsInvertible (Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M M') := by
767	  obtain ⟨N, ⟨e⟩⟩ := hM
768	  obtain ⟨N', ⟨e'⟩⟩ := hM'
769	  exact ⟨Scheme.Modules.tensorObj N N',
770	    ⟨tensorObj_middleFour M M' N N' ≪≫ tensorObjIsoOfIso e e' ≪≫ tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
771	
772	/-- **Step 4 — the structure sheaf is `⊗`-invertible** (blueprint
773	`lem:isinvertible_unit`). Witness `𝒪_X`, iso `tensorObj_unit_iso`. -/
774	theorem isInvertible_unit {X : Scheme.{u}} :
775	    IsInvertible (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :=
776	  ⟨SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, ⟨tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
777	
778	/-- **Step 5 — the tensor inverse is determined up to isomorphism** (blueprint
779	`lem:isinvertible_inverse_welldef`). If `M ⊗ N ≅ 𝒪_X` and `M ⊗ N' ≅ 𝒪_X` then
780	`N ≅ N'`, via the inverse-of-inverse chain. -/
781	theorem IsInvertible.inverse_unique {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N N' : X.Modules}
782	    (e : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
783	    (e' : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N' ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
784	    Nonempty (N ≅ N') :=
785	  ⟨(tensorObj_right_unitor N).symm ≪≫
786	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl N) e'.symm ≪≫
787	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
788	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding N M ≪≫ e) (Iso.refl N') ≪≫
789	    tensorObj_left_unitor N'⟩
790	
791	/-- The setoid of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules: `M ∼ M'` iff there exists an
792	isomorphism `M ≅ M'` (blueprint `def:pic_carrier`). -/
793	instance picSetoid (X : Scheme.{u}) : Setoid {M : X.Modules // IsInvertible M} where
794	  r M M' := Nonempty ((M : X.Modules) ≅ (M' : X.Modules))
795	  iseqv :=
796	    ⟨fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ ⟨f⟩ => ⟨e ≪≫ f⟩⟩
797	
798	/-- **Step 2 — the invertibility-carrier Picard group** (blueprint
799	`def:pic_carrier`): the quotient of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules by isomorphism. -/
800	def PicGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ := Quotient (picSetoid X)
801	
802	/-- Multiplication on `PicGroup X`: `[M] · [M'] := [M ⊗_X M']`, well-defined on
803	iso-classes by bifunctoriality (`tensorObjIsoOfIso`), landing in `PicGroup` by
804	`IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
805	noncomputable def picMul {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X → PicGroup X → PicGroup X :=
806	  Quotient.lift₂
807	    (fun a b => Quotient.mk _ ⟨tensorObj a.1 b.1, a.2.tensorObj b.2⟩)
808	    (by
809	      rintro ⟨a, ha⟩ ⟨b, hb⟩ ⟨a', ha'⟩ ⟨b', hb'⟩ ⟨ea⟩ ⟨eb⟩
810	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObjIsoOfIso ea eb⟩)
811	
812	/-- The inverse class on `PicGroup X`: `[M] ↦ [N]` for the membership witness `N`
813	of `IsInvertible M`, well-defined by `IsInvertible.inverse_unique`. -/
814	noncomputable def picInv {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X → PicGroup X :=
815	  Quotient.lift
816	    (fun a => Quotient.mk _
817	      ⟨Classical.choose a.2,
818	        a.1, ⟨tensorObj_braiding _ a.1 ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec a.2).some⟩⟩)
819	    (by
820	      rintro ⟨a, ha⟩ ⟨a', ha'⟩ ⟨ea⟩
821	      refine Quotient.sound ?_
822	      -- both `Classical.choose ha` and `Classical.choose ha'` are tensor inverses of `a`
823	      have h1 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
824	        (Classical.choose_spec ha).some
825	      have h2 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha') ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
826	        tensorObjIsoOfIso ea (Iso.refl _) ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec ha').some
827	      exact IsInvertible.inverse_unique h1 h2)

830	`thm:pic_commgroup`). `[M] · [M'] := [M ⊗_X M']`, `1 := [𝒪_X]`, and `[M]⁻¹` the

832	existence-of-isomorphism: unit laws ← unitors, associativity ← associator,
833	commutativity ← braiding, left inverse ← the witness iso. No monoidal coherence. -/
834	noncomputable instance picCommGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : CommGroup (PicGroup X) where
835	  mul := picMul

848	  mul_one := by
849	    rintro a
850	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
851	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_right_unitor a.1⟩
852	  inv_mul_cancel := by

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate

open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance

noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  @asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
    (isIso_pbu_of_final g)

@[simp] lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso_hom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    (pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g).hom =
      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom := rfl

noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso f

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

/-! ### Phase 1 — `pullbackUnitIso` (`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`), blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`

**iter-241 RESOLUTION (the chart-chase is NOT needed).** The unit comparison
`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism for *every* morphism of
schemes `f`, not just for `Final`-chart morphisms. The Mathlib instance
`SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal` fires whenever the comparison
functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`, and that functor is **always** `Final`: the
preimage functor on opens preserves finite limits (it is a frame homomorphism), so it is
representably flat, whence `final_of_representablyFlat` supplies `(Opens.map f.base).Final`
unconditionally. (Verified axiom-clean for a general `f`.) The elaborate affine
chart-chase contemplated by the blueprint proof — and the iter-240 coherence linchpin
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` — are therefore unnecessary for this lemma (the linchpin is
retained above as it is the genuine Mathlib-absent pseudofunctor coherence, of independent
use for the harder Phase-2 tensor comparison).

The remaining friction was purely a Lean typeclass-resolution accident: in a context with
several `(Opens.map _).Final` hypotheses (or after a `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` rewrite)
the buried implicit instance args of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (`[F.IsContinuous]`,
`[(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]`) are defeq-but-not-syntactic, so `asIso`/`infer_instance`
fails to synthesise `IsIso (pbu f)`. The fix (mathlib-analogist `pbu-canon`, the
`Functor.Monoidal.μIso` idiom): isolate the single `Final` hypothesis in the helper
`isIso_pbu_of_final` whose body `inferInstance` runs at a clean site, then transport the
resulting witness through `asIso` by passing it *explicitly* (`@asIso … (isIso_pbu_of_final g)`)
— the application's defeq check runs at default transparency and succeeds, whereas instance
synthesis (reducible transparency) does not. -/

/-- **`IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit g)` from a single `Final` hypothesis, at a clean site.**
Project-local: isolates the lone `(Opens.map g.base).Final` instance so that the Mathlib
`OfFinal` instance synthesises without colliding with other in-scope `Final`/`IsIso`
hypotheses (see the section note). -/
private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance

/-- **Bundled `Iso` form of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g`** for a `Final`
comparison functor — the analogue of `CategoryTheory.Functor.Monoidal.μIso`. Project-local:
hands out the isomorphism (rather than a bare `IsIso` instance) so downstream coherence
reasoning stays at the `Iso` level and never re-triggers the `pbu` instance synthesis. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  @asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
    (isIso_pbu_of_final g)

@[simp] lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso_hom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
    (pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g).hom =
      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom := rfl

/-- **Pullback preserves the structure sheaf** (blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`):
`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` for an arbitrary morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X`, where
`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`. The comparison functor `Opens.map f.base` is
always `Final` (preimage on opens is representably flat), so the Mathlib unit comparison
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism unconditionally. -/
noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso f

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (4, all axiom-clean):**
  - `isIso_pbu_of_final` (private lemma) — line ~1036
  - `pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso` (def) — line ~1047
  - `pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso_hom` (`@[simp]` lemma) — line ~1054
  - `pullbackUnitIso` (def, **blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`**) — line ~1066
- **Declarations blocked (0 attempted-and-failed):** Phase 2 (`pullbackTensorIso`) and
  Phase 3 (`IsInvertible.pullback`) NOT added — Phase 2 is a genuine Mathlib-absent build
  (no comparison map, no monoidal structure on `SheafOfModules`), Phase 3 depends on it.
- **Sorry count (whole file): 2 → 2** (unchanged). The two sorries are the pre-existing
  deferred dual-bridge ones (`exists_tensorObj_inverse` L715, `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`
  L1205) — NOT touched, per directive. **No new sorry. `pullbackUnitIso` is a NEW
  canonical sorry-free declaration on the critical path.**

## MAJOR FINDING (changes the strategy for the whole §6 lane)

**`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism for EVERY morphism of
schemes `f` — not only for `Final`-chart morphisms.** The Mathlib instance
`instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal` requires `(Opens.map f.base).Final`, and that
holds **unconditionally**: the preimage functor on opens preserves finite limits (frame
homomorphism), hence is representably flat, so `CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat`
supplies `(Opens.map f.base).Final` for any `f`. Verified axiom-clean for a general `f`
via `lean_run_code` (`#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`).

**Consequence:** the elaborate affine chart-chase in the blueprint proof of
`lem:pullback_unit_iso` (and the iter-240 coherence linchpin `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`)
are NOT needed for the unit iso. `pullbackUnitIso f := pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso f` after
`haveI : (Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _`. The blueprint proof
prose is now over-engineered (BLUEPRINT FLAG for plan/review: the chart-chase proof of
`lem:pullback_unit_iso` can be replaced by the one-line representable-flatness argument;
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` is retained as genuine Mathlib-absent pseudofunctor coherence
— keep it, it is the most plausible ingredient for the harder Phase-2 tensor comparison).

## pullbackUnitIso (blueprint lem:pullback_unit_iso) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach:** `Opens.map f.base` is always `Final` (representable flatness) ⟹ `pbu f`
  iso ⟹ bundle as `Iso`. **READY FOR `\leanok`** on `lem:pullback_unit_iso`.
- **The TC accident the analogist diagnosed was real** but only a proof-tactic issue:
  with several `(Opens.map _).Final` (or after a `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` rewrite) in
  scope, the buried implicit instance args of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
  (`[F.IsContinuous]`, `[(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]`) are defeq-but-not-syntactic, so
  `asIso`/`infer_instance` fails to synthesise `IsIso (pbu f)` (reducible transparency).
- **The fix (reusable idiom, per `analogies/pbu-canon.md`):**
  1. `isIso_pbu_of_final g [(Opens.map g.base).Final] : IsIso (pbu g) := inferInstance` —
     isolates the lone `Final` so `inferInstance` runs at a clean site.
  2. `pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g := @asIso _ _ _ _ (pbu g) (isIso_pbu_of_final g)` —
     pass the witness EXPLICITLY; the `@asIso` application's typecheck is `isDefEq` at
     **default** transparency, which succeeds even though instance synthesis (reducible)
     does not. This is the `Functor.Monoidal.μIso` bundled-iso idiom the analogist named.
- **GOTCHAS (record for Phase 2):**
  - `asIso (pbu …)` and `Functor.map_isIso _ _ (pbu …)` FAIL to synthesise `IsIso (pbu …)`
    even with a type-ascribed `haveI : IsIso (pbu …)` in scope — the in-scope witness and
    the freshly-elaborated `pbu …` differ in buried implicits at reducible transparency.
    Always go through `@asIso … (witness)` / pass instances explicitly.
  - `have t : IsIso (pbu g) := inferInstance` (type-ascribed) WORKS (expected type pins
    the implicits before synthesis). The asymmetry vs `asIso` is pure elaboration order.

## isIso_pbu_of_final / pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso / _hom — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- Reusable bricks. `pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso` is the bundled `Iso` (μIso analogue) the
  analogist recommended; ready for Phase 2's `pullbackObjTensorToTensor`.

## ALSO PROVEN BUT NOT COMMITTED — the full chart-chase per-chart lemma
A complete axiom-clean `isIso_restrict_pbu (f) (U V) (hVU : V ≤ f⁻¹ᵁU) :
IsIso ((restrictFunctor V.ι).map (pbu f))` was built and verified (uses the iter-240
linchpin `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` twice + `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`). It is the
blueprint's intended route but is **made redundant** by the representable-flatness finding,
so it was NOT committed (dead code). The recipe, if ever needed for a finality-style
argument elsewhere:
- factor `V.ι ≫ f = g ≫ U.ι` via `g := f.resLE U V hVU` (`resLE_comp_ι`);
- `pbu (g ≫ U.ι)` iso = composite `(pullbackComp g U.ι).app.symm ≪≫ (pullback g).mapIso
  (pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso U.ι) ≪≫ pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g`, transfer via `.isIso_hom`;
- `rw [hg_comp, pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp V.ι f]`, cancel the outer legs by passing the
  composite witness to `@IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left … ha hgU_iso` then
  `@IsIso.of_isIso_comp_right … hc hbc` (infer the middle from the CANONICAL witness type,
  never a fresh ascription), then `(NatIso.isIso_map_iff (restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι) _).mpr`.

## Phase 2 — pullbackTensorIso (f^*(M⊗N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N) — NOT ADDED, genuine blocker
- **Approach considered + searched:** Confirmed via filesystem grep of Mathlib that there
  is **no** tensor pullback comparison and **no** `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules …)`
  (only `PresheafOfModules/Monoidal.lean`). `PullbackFree.lean` provides only
  `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (unit) and `pullbackObjFreeIso` (free modules) — no tensor.
- **Why blocked:** the project's `tensorObj M N = sheafify(M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)` and
  `f^* = Scheme.Modules.pullback f` is an ABSTRACT left adjoint (`(pushforward).leftAdjoint`)
  with no sectionwise/stalkwise value, so there is no canonical map to even state the iso
  at the sheaf level; the unit-case `OfFinal` trick does NOT generalise (no tensor analogue
  of `instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal`).
- **Precise next-step decomposition (a multi-hundred-LOC build, own sub-lane):**
  1. Build a comparison `pullbackObjTensorToTensor : f^*(tensorObj M N) ⟶ tensorObj (f^*M)(f^*N)`
     via sheafification reconciliation: `f^*(sheafify P) ≅ sheafify(presheaf-pullback P)`
     (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`) + presheaf-level monoidality of the
     presheaf pullback + the landed `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` (RHS reconciliation, ~L884).
  2. Prove it iso — the genuine content (extension of scalars is STRONG monoidal,
     restriction only lax). Likely needs a sectionwise/stalkwise identification of the
     abstract presheaf pullback with `extendScalars`, which is itself Mathlib-absent at the
     pinned commit, OR a finality-chart-chase mirroring `isIso_restrict_pbu` above once a
     comparison map exists.
- **Mathlib-bump note:** worth checking whether a post-2026-05-31 bump adds a monoidal
  pullback for `(Presheaf|Sheaf)OfModules`; that would collapse Phase 2.

## Phase 3 — IsInvertible.pullback — NOT ADDED, gated on Phase 2
- Route (once Phase 2 lands): given `⟨N, e : tensorObj M N ≅ 𝒪_X⟩`, take `f^*N` as the
  inverse; `tensorObj (f^*M)(f^*N) ≅[pullbackTensorIso.symm] f^*(tensorObj M N)
  ≅[(pullback f).mapIso e] f^*𝒪_X ≅[pullbackUnitIso] 𝒪_Y`. Iso-level by construction
  (no recurrence of the TC accident). `pullbackUnitIso` (this iter) supplies the last leg.

## Why I stopped
**Real progress:** the PRIMARY objective (Phase 1, `pullbackUnitIso`) is closed
axiom-clean as a canonical sorry-free declaration (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso`),
plus 3 reusable bricks (`isIso_pbu_of_final`, `pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso`,
`pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso_hom`). I additionally established that the chart-chase the
blueprint demanded is unnecessary (representable-flatness finding), which de-risks and
simplifies the lane. Phase 2 (`pullbackTensorIso`) is a genuine Mathlib-absent build with
no comparison map at the sheaf level — confirmed by Mathlib grep and consistent with the
prior handoff; it is the real remaining cost and cannot be closed cleanly this session
without risking a sorry. Phase 3 is gated on Phase 2. Decomposition for both handed off
above. Informal agent not consulted: per CLAUDE.md the `MOONSHOT` key was 401 last iter and
the blocker is a build-size issue (Mathlib-absent infra), not a proof-sketch gap.

## Blueprint markers (for review agent)
- `lem:pullback_unit_iso` → ready for `\leanok` (sync_leanok will confirm).
- `lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`, `lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp` remain `\leanok`
  (untouched, still axiom-clean).
- FLAG: the prose proof of `lem:pullback_unit_iso` (the chart-chase) is now over-engineered
  vs. the one-line representable-flatness argument actually used; plan agent may simplify.

I've completed this iteration. Here's the summary:

## Result: Phase 1 (PRIMARY) closed axiom-clean

**`pullbackUnitIso` (`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`, blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`) is now a sorry-free, axiom-clean declaration** on the critical path, plus 3 reusable bricks (`isIso_pbu_of_final`, `pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso`, `pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso_hom`). Verified `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. File still has exactly the 2 pre-existing deferred sorries — **no new sorry**.

### Major finding (simplifies the whole lane)
`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism for **every** scheme morphism — not just `Final`-chart ones. The comparison functor `Opens.map f.base` is always `Final` (preimage-on-opens preserves finite limits ⟹ representably flat ⟹ `final_of_representablyFlat`). So the elaborate affine chart-chase the blueprint demanded was **unnecessary**; `pullbackUnitIso` is a one-liner.

### The TC accident the analogist diagnosed (resolved)
`asIso (pbu g)` / `infer_instance` fail to synthesise `IsIso (pbu g)` (buried implicits defeq-but-not-syntactic at *reducible* transparency). Fix = the `μIso` bundled-iso idiom: isolate the lone `Final` in a helper, then `@asIso _ _ _ _ (pbu g) (isIso_pbu_of_final g)` — passing the witness explicitly makes the typecheck succeed at *default* transparency.

### Phase 2/3 — genuine Mathlib-absent blocker (documented, not added)
Confirmed by Mathlib grep: no tensor-pullback comparison and no `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules)` exist. `pullbackTensorIso` requires building a bespoke comparison map (`sheafificationCompPullback` + presheaf-pullback monoidality + the landed `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`) and proving it iso — a multi-hundred-LOC sub-lane. Phase 3 (`IsInvertible.pullback`) is gated on it; full route handed off in `task_results/TensorObjSubstrate.lean.md`.

I flagged for the plan/review agents that the blueprint's chart-chase proof prose of `lem:pullback_unit_iso` is now over-engineered relative to the one-line representable-flatness argument actually used.
